pl-v-minister-of-employment-and-social-d.lesson: Pro-se fabricated-authority rulings abroad strengthen our argument that verification duties are universal.

chan-v-british-columbia-2025-11-28.lesson: Courts now order security-for-costs when AI-fabricated citations surface — verify before filing to avoid cost exposure.

elías-axel-roberto-rafael-s-abuso-sexual.lesson: Uncritical AI use in a criminal defense brief can cost you the case and your bar standing — require human-supervised review.

lessard-c-longuépée-2025-11-26.lesson: ChatGPT-generated citations continue to draw public warnings — document any AI use and verify in primary sources.

oxford-hotel-investments-ltd-v-great-yar.lesson: UK tribunals now call out AI-generated misquotations by name — treat false quotes as seriously as fake cases.

re-walker-2025-11-24.lesson: Using legal-tech tools like CourtAid alongside ChatGPT does not excuse verification — reprimands follow regardless of tool pedigree.

walker-v-collingwood-general-and-marine-.lesson: Reconsideration denials citing AI-invented precedent reinforce that every cite must resolve on CanLII before filing.

syndicat-des-travailleuses-et-travailleu.lesson: Arbitration panels now publish warnings for hallucinated citations — informality of the forum is no defense.

mark-jennings-v-natwest-group-plc-2025-1.lesson: Scottish appellate courts are now naming ChatGPT submissions as unreliable — expect the same scrutiny for counsel.

rr-v-fraser-health-authority-and-others-.lesson: Opposing counsel can now replicate AI hallucinations by re-prompting — build an audit trail proving your cites are human-verified.

wang-v-mongeon-2025-11-19.lesson: Tribunals cross-check citation numbers against their own docket — invented HRTO numbers are exposed within one filing.

dj-v-sn-2025-11-19.lesson: Admission of AI use at hearing now draws cost orders — counsel who conceal face far worse.

eclinlrbnne20254814-2025-11-19.lesson: Dutch courts are cross-checking ECLI numbers and flagging ChatGPT-sourced citations — verify every ECLI on rechtspraak.nl.

yuehong-v-the-minister-of-citizenship-im.lesson: Federal Court will refuse to anonymize counsel who file AI-hallucinated cites — expect your name in the published opinion.

ms-j-m-jain-prop-sh-jeetmal-choraria-v-u.lesson: Indian High Courts now physically verify reporters against cited volumes — wrong ITR cites draw public warnings against AI misuse.

kim-v-insurance-australia-2025-11-18.lesson: Even judges get their decisions pulled for AI hallucinations — require second-set verification for every cited authority.

small-moves-canada-inc-v-garner-2025-11-.lesson: AI can invent entire statutes, not just cases — confirm every named Act exists on the official legislature site before pleading.

133-blackstock-road-v-assethold-limited-.lesson: UK property tribunals now issue show-cause orders when authorities cannot be produced — treat failure to supply a cite as sanction-triggering.
